name: subagent-orchestration
description:
This skill should be used when coordinating multiple subagents, implementing orchestrator
patterns, or managing parallel agent workflows.
Trigger phrases: "orchestrate agents", "coordinate subagents", "parallel agents",
"multi-agent workflow", "delegate to agents", "run agents in parallel", "launch multiple agents".
capabilities: Define clear scopes for subagent tasks with explicit file lists
Specify files that subagents must NOT modify
Coordinate parallel execution of independent subagent tasks
Create task templates with constraints and business rules
Reference documentation using @file syntax for subagent context
author: DieGopherLT
securitySummary: This skill is a pure documentation resource containing only markdown guidelines for coordinating subagent workflows. No code execution, network access, file operations, or external command capabilities exist. All 28 static findings are false positives caused by pattern matching on benign text like 'orchestrate' and workflow terminology. This is not executable code.
valueStatement: Managing multiple AI subagents can lead to conflicting changes and scope creep. This skill provides structured guidelines for orchestrating parallel agent workflows with clear boundaries, file scopes, and task isolation.
userTitle: Coordinate Multiple AI Subagents in Parallel
limitations: Does not execute code - only provides coordination guidelines
Does not directly invoke subagents - recommendations for users
Requires users to define task scopes manually
Does not enforce task boundaries automatically

useCases.title: Parallel Code Reviews
useCases.description: Run multiple subagents to review different modules of a codebase simultaneously

useCases.title: Independent Feature Work
useCases.description: Coordinate subagents to work on separate features without conflicts

useCases.title: Distributed Analysis
useCases.description: Analyze different system components in parallel for architectural decisions

promptTemplates.title: Basic Subagent Request
promptTemplates.prompt: orchestrate agents: Task: [description]. Files to modify: [list]. Files NOT to touch: [list]. Constraints: [rules].

promptTemplates.title: Parallel Tasks
promptTemplates.prompt: launch multiple agents in parallel: Agent 1: Task [A], modify [files], don't touch [files]. Agent 2: Task [B], modify [files], don't touch [files].

promptTemplates.title: With Documentation
promptTemplates.prompt: coordinate subagents: Task [X]. Reference docs: [@file1, @file2]. Files: [modify], [exclude]. Constraints: [requirements].

promptTemplates.title: Full Scope Definition
promptTemplates.prompt: orchestrate agents with scope: Task [name]. Files to modify: [exact paths]. Files NOT to touch: [specific exclusions]. Business rules: [list]. Technical requirements: [list]. Reference: [@docs].

bestPractices: Always define explicit file boundaries for each subagent
Specify files NOT to touch to prevent unintended modifications
Use @file references to provide subagents with relevant context
antiPatterns: Vague file specifications like 'update related files'
Missing exclusions that allow subagents to modify unrelated code
Sequential dependencies disguised as parallel tasks

faq.question: What triggers this skill?
faq.answer: Use trigger phrases like 'orchestrate agents', 'coordinate subagents', 'parallel agents', 'launch multiple agents', or 'delegate to agents'.

faq.question: Can this skill run subagents automatically?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides guidelines for users to define and coordinate subagent tasks with proper scope boundaries.

faq.question: How does parallel execution differ from sequential?
faq.answer: Parallel tasks are completely independent with no shared dependencies. Sequential tasks require results from previous tasks before starting.

faq.question: Is my data safe when using this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill only provides guidance. No data is sent externally. Subagents run within your controlled environment.

faq.question: What if subagent scopes overlap?
faq.answer: Avoid overlapping scopes. Define exclusive file lists for each subagent. Use 'Files NOT to touch' to enforce boundaries.

faq.question: How does this compare to single-agent workflows?
faq.answer: This skill enables faster completion of independent tasks by running subagents in parallel. Single-agent workflows handle sequential or dependent tasks.
